title: Today's Markets Brief
summary: Rising inflation may force Fed to tighten sooner than markets expect, FedEx Freight set for market debut

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and I'm taking a look at the latest news in the finance world. We've got a couple of interesting stories to share today. Inflation is on the rise, and it's got everyone talking about what the Fed might do next. Meanwhile, FedEx Freight is getting ready to make its market debut. It's always exciting to see new players enter the market, and this one could be worth watching.

According to a recent report, rising inflation may force the Fed to tighten sooner than markets expect. This is a story from Investing.com, and it's based on comments from Yardeni. The idea here is that if inflation keeps going up, the Fed might have to step in and adjust interest rates to keep things under control. It's a classic balancing act - the Fed wants to keep the economy growing, but it also wants to make sure inflation doesn't get out of hand. I've been following this story, and it's interesting to see how the market is reacting to the possibility of tighter monetary policy.

Also on the board, FedEx Freight is set to make its market debut as it completes its spinoff. This is a big move for the company, and it's worth keeping an eye on. The spinoff is nearing completion, and FedEx Freight is getting ready to start trading on its own. I'm curious to see how this will play out - will FedEx Freight be a major player in the market, or will it be a smaller fish in a big pond?

My quick read: today's market story has that familiar split-screen feeling. There is real excitement around artificial intelligence and chips, but the room still keeps one eye on risk, because markets enjoy a celebration more when they know where the exits are.

sources.title: Rising inflation may force Fed to tighten sooner than markets expect: Yardeni
